resources.string:
This feature reads your ES-DE library and launches games directly from the home screen.

Not every emulator is supported yet — if yours isn't working, please open a GitHub issue so it can be added.

GitHub: github.com/BrianJr03/Jarngreipr

ROM Search reads your ES-DE game library and lets you launch games directly from the home screen. Tap the controller icon to open it, then type a game name, system (e.g. @psp), or genre to filter results.
My emulator isn\'t supported — what do I do?

Not every emulator is supported on day one. When picking an emulator for a game, use the \"Choose App\" option at the bottom of the list to launch with any installed app as a workaround. If you\'d like full support added, please open a GitHub issue at github.com/BrianJr03/Jarngreipr.
Allows the Home Launcher to open recent apps screen
Tap this icon to switch between vertical (alphabetical keyboard) and horizontal (QWERTY keyboard) layouts.
Add this app to your home page
No icons found in this pack
• Broadcasts your active theme to nearby devices over Bluetooth
• Both devices must have sharing active at the same time
• Set a display name so others know who sent a theme
• Tap a received theme to apply and save it
• Disable battery optimization for reliable background use
You can revisit this in FAQ
No themes received yet.\nBring a nearby device running this app within BLE range to receive themes.
A new system background was received via Nearby
Broadcast current wallpaper automatically on launch
Transfer failed — tap Send to retry
Broadcast current theme automatically on launch
Keep keyboard keys styled when OLED mode is on
Display names below icons in the app drawer
Display names below icons on the home screen
Adjust the font size of app name labels
Display back button on all screens
☕ Get early builds & support the project
Choose which apps to show or hide
Show newly installed apps on home screen automatically
%1$d app can be displayed. %2$d selected app will be hidden.
%1$d apps can be displayed. %2$d selected apps will be hidden.
%1$d app can be displayed. All selected apps will be visible.
%1$d apps can be displayed. All selected apps will be visible.
All %1$d selected apps will be displayed.
Select what happens when you press the back button
Select what happens when you double tap the logo
Configure logo visibility and overlay mode per tab
Show game description as overlay. Not supported yet.
Display the logo when browsing game systems
Display the logo when browsing games within a system
Tap on a widget to swap positions
Use the + and - buttons below to adjust size
Disable vertical scrolling on this tab.
Open app drawer by flinging to the bottom on this tab.
Only show apps visible on the current tab when opening the app drawer.
Pull down from the top of any tab to reveal the Now Playing shade.
See what changed in this version
If the bottom keyboard doesn\'t appear when requested from the top screen, tap the bottom screen and then try again.
How can I prevent the Android system from closing Jarngreipr?
Open the Recent Apps screen and swipe down on Jarngreipr. This will \'lock\' the app and tell Android to never close Jarngreipr.
Why doesn\'t the keyboard appear when I request it?
If the bottom keyboard doesn\'t appear when requested from the top screen, tap the bottom screen first and then try again. This ensures the bottom screen is in focus to receive the keyboard input.

Theme Sharing broadcasts your active theme to nearby devices over Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE). Both devices must have the app open with sharing active at the same time.

Set a display name so others can identify your themes. When a theme is received, it appears in the Theme Sharing screen — tap it to apply and save it.

For reliable background sharing, disable battery optimization for this app in your device settings.

Wallpaper Sharing uses the Nearby Connections API to send and receive wallpapers between nearby devices over Wi-Fi and Bluetooth.

Enable Nearby Wallpaper in the Theme Share screen to start discovering devices. When a wallpaper is received, it appears in the Received Wallpapers list where you can apply or delete it.

Both devices need to have Nearby Wallpaper active and the required Bluetooth and Wi-Fi permissions granted.
How can I use MacroDroid (or similar) to toggle wallpaper modes?

You can trigger wallpaper mode switches from any automation app (e.g. MacroDroid, Tasker) by sending a broadcast intent.\n\nIn MacroDroid, add a Send Intent action, set Type to Broadcast, and fill in these fields:

Jingles are short audio clips that play automatically when you browse games in ES-DE. When you select a game, Jarngreipr looks up a matching Jingle and plays it in the background, giving each game its own sound.\n\nJingle collections are defined by a simple index.json file that maps game titles to audio files. You can use community repos from GitHub or build your own local collection.

Jarngreipr uses three source types, each with different network behaviour:

• Downloaded repos: Tap the download icon on a GitHub repo card to save all audio files to your device. Once downloaded, playback never requires a network connection.

• Local folders: Pick any folder on your device containing an index.json and a jingles/ subfolder. These always play offline via direct file access.

• Streaming repos: GitHub repos that have not been downloaded stream audio directly from GitHub. These require an active internet connection each time a Jingle plays and there may be delay before playback.
How does the app know which Jingle to play for a game?

When you browse a game in ES-DE, Jarngreipr receives the game's filename. It strips the path and extension, then searches every loaded index.json for an entry whose game field matches.

Matching is case-insensitive and works both ways. A file named "Super Mario Bros. (USA).zip" will match an entry titled "Super Mario Bros.", and vice versa. The first match found across all your sources wins.

If no match is found the currently playing Jingle stops and nothing new plays.
What format does index.json need to be in?

Your index.json must live at the root of your folder or GitHub repo. The name field sets the display name shown in the app. Each entry maps a game title to an audio file path relative to the root. Supported formats are anything ExoPlayer can decode — MP3, OGG, FLAC, and WAV all work well.
My Jingle isn\'t playing — what should I check?

Work through these checks in order:

1. Make sure the Enable Jingles toggle is on at the top of the Jingles screen.

2. Confirm ES-DE is actually sending game selection events to Jarngreipr (the app must be running).

3. Check that your game's filename (without extension) matches a game entry in your index.json. Partial matches are supported.

4. If using a local folder, tap the refresh button on the folder card after editing index.json. Changes are not picked up automatically.

5. If streaming from GitHub, verify you have an active internet connection. Download the repo for fully offline playback.

6. Ensure the json file does not have any trailing commas.
Can I set a fallback Jingle for an entire platform or for every game?

Yes — two special game names let you define fallback Jingles:

• "global-default-jingle" — plays for any game that has no other match. Add exactly one entry with this name across your packs; the first one found wins.

• "system-jingle" — plays for all games on a specific platform. The platform is determined by the folder name ES-DE reports (e.g. "n3ds", "wii"). In a grouped index.json the platform key is used automatically. In a flat index.json add a "platform" field to the entry.

Both fall back naturally: if a per-game Jingle is found it is always preferred.
Thor is the Norse god of thunder, lightning, storms, and strength.
Thor wields Mjolnir, a hammer so powerful it can level mountains.
Thursday is named after Thor, derived from Thor\'s Day.
Thor rides a chariot pulled by two goats named Tanngrisnir and Tanngnjóstr.
Thor is the son of Odin, the All-Father, and Jord, the personification of Earth.
Mjolnir always returns to Thor\'s hand after being thrown.
Thor\'s wife is Sif, known for her golden hair that shines like wheat fields.
Thor is said to have immense strength, even among the gods.
Thor\'s hammer Mjolnir was forged by the dwarven brothers Brokkr and Eitri.
Thor protects both gods and humans from the giants.
Thor owns a belt called Megingjord that doubles his already incredible strength.
Thor is one of the most popular and widely worshipped gods in Norse mythology.
Thor\'s hall in Asgard is called Bilskirnir, which has 540 rooms.
Thor once disguised himself as the goddess Freyja to retrieve Mjolnir from giants.
Thor\'s red beard is one of his most distinctive features in Norse tales.
Thor is prophesied to die fighting the serpent Jormungandr during Ragnarok.
Thor uses iron gloves called Járngreipr to wield Mjolnir.
Thor is known for his hearty appetite and can consume entire oxen.
Thor once tried to drink the ocean in a challenge from a giant king.
Thor\'s mother Jord is the earth itself, making Thor part divine and part elemental.
Thor has two sons, Modi and Magni, who will survive Ragnarok.
Thor is often depicted as having fierce eyes that glow when angered.
Thor\'s goats can be eaten and then brought back to life the next day.
Thor is the defender of Midgard, the realm of humans.
Thor has a daughter named Thrud, whose name means strength.
Thor is second only to Odin in the Norse pantheon hierarchy.
Thor\'s hammer Mjolnir is so heavy that only he can lift it.
Thor once lifted a cat that was actually the Midgard Serpent in disguise.
Thor travels between worlds using the rainbow bridge Bifrost.
Thor is known for his straightforward, honest nature and quick temper.
Thor\'s symbol, the hammer, was worn by Vikings for protection.
Thor once wrestled an old woman who was actually Old Age personified.
Thor is associated with oak trees, which are sacred to him.
Thor\'s name means thunder in Old Norse.
Thor is known to have a simple, direct approach to problem-solving.
Thor\'s hammer makes the sound of thunder when striking.
Thor once fought and defeated countless giants single-handedly.
Thor is the god most commonly invoked for protection by common folk.
Thor\'s strength is so great he must wear iron gloves to handle Mjolnir.
Thor is destined to kill the Midgard Serpent but die from its venom.
Thor\'s chariot creates thunder when riding across the sky.
Thor is married to Sif in one of Norse mythology\'s most stable marriages.
Thor\'s hall Bilskirnir is the largest building in existence.
Thor once drank so much that he lowered the sea level noticeably.
Thor represents the common warrior rather than the noble or wise leader.
Thor\'s stepson is Ullr, the god of skiing and archery.
Thor is said to have a voracious appetite matching his legendary strength.
Thor\'s adventures often involve outwitting or outfighting giants.
Thor is the protector of the consecration of marriages and sacred oaths.
Thor will take nine steps after slaying the serpent before falling dead.
Are you sure you want to delete Page %1$d? All widgets on this page will be removed.
Apps only. Display apps in grid or free positioning.
Apps and widgets. Display apps in grid only.
Empty tab with an app drawer. Swipe up to open the drawer.
A tab for browsing RSS feeds.
Access app settings, themes, wallpapers, and customization options.
Shows current page position. Click to quickly change your Home tab.
Access quick actions and page-specific options.
On the first tab, press (or gesture) Back to toggle the on-screen keyboard.
Tap the + button to add apps
Remove this app from the page
Toggle edit controls for all widgets
Quickly delete files you no longer need
Tap the button below to add a folder
Select apps to add to this folder
Reset app positions and icon sizes
Drag around and resize app icons
Prevent app icons from being dragged
Allow app icons to be dragged
Lock app positions to enable long press.
No apps are currently visible on this tab. Tap the button below to add apps.
This tab is empty. Tap the button below to add apps or widgets.
1. Install Shizuku from the Play Store or GitHub
2. Open Shizuku and follow the setup instructions
1. Install Termux from the Play Store or GitHub
2. Go to Settings → Developer Options → Wireless Debugging
3. Enable Wireless Debugging and tap \"Pair device with pairing code\"
4. In Termux, run: pkg install android-tools
7. Run command shown in Shizuku app to start it
1. Return here and tap \"Permission required\" to grant access
2. Tap \"Edit\" to customize button mappings and sensitivity
3. Once status shows \"Ready\", you\'re all set
Shizuku provides the elevated permissions needed to inject gamepad inputs into other apps. It must be running for GamePad to work, and you\'ll need to restart it after rebooting your device.
Some emulators may not respond to injected inputs because they only detect physical gamepad hardware. Native Android apps and games typically work best.
View crash reports or report an issue
Dual-screen volume management for Ayn Thor
No apps on your device provide widgets.
View and manage app crash reports
This will permanently delete all %1$d crash log(s).
Your app hasn\'t crashed recently.\nCrash logs will appear here if the app encounters an error.
• Hidden: Jarngreipr will be hidden in task switcher
Please wait while the update is being downloaded…
The update is ready to be installed. Tap Install to continue.
To install updates directly, please allow this app to install apps from unknown sources in the settings.
You can now download and install updates directly.
Your recently used apps will appear here.
To view recent apps, please grant usage access permission.
Use Termux (optional) or connect via USB to run ADB commands
After granting permission, return here to use the volume sliders
Secondary screen volume control requires WRITE_SECURE_SETTINGS permission. This is a system-level permission that can only be granted via ADB.
Show notification counts on app icons
Grant notification access to show notification count badges on app icons. This helps you see which apps have unread notifications at a glance.

Since this app was installed outside the Play Store, you need to allow restricted settings first:

1. Tap "Open App Settings" below
2. Tap the ⋮ menu (top right)
3. Select "Allow restricted settings"
4. Return here and tap "Grant Access"
Customize your dock appearance and behavior
Tap to select, then tap another app to swap
Add a new empty slot to the dock (%1$d/%2$d slots)
Select how many apps can be added to the dock
Choose which tabs display the dock
Floating button to open app drawer on scrollable tabs
Choose which tabs display the button

Before using ES-DE with Jarngreipr, you'll need to follow these steps

1. Grant storage permissions
2. Select ES-DE scripts folder
3. Create script files
4. Select downloaded media folder
5. Enable scripts in ES-DE

If you're familiar with ES-DE Companion, this is the exact same process.
Storage access is required to create scripts and access media.\n\nTap \'Grant Permission\' to open settings. The wizard will continue once granted.
The app can now manage scripts and media files.
Select your ES-DE scripts folder (typically ~/ES-DE/scripts).\n\nTap \'Select Folder\' to choose, or \'Use Default\' for the standard internal storage path.
Now we will create the necessary integration scripts in your selected folder.\n\nTap \'Create Scripts\' to proceed, or \'Skip\' if they already exist.

Select your ES-DE downloaded_media folder.

Default path:
/ES-DE/downloaded_media

Tap 'Select Folder' to choose, or 'Use Default' for internal storage.

Final step! Enable these settings in ES-DE

1. Open ES-DE Menu (START)
2. Go to Other Settings
3. Toggle ON 'Custom Event Scripts'
4. Toggle ON 'Browsing Custom Events'
You can now switch between ES-DE and any other type of wallpaper.\n\nShout out to @RobZombie9043 for ES-DE Companion and the beauty of open source :)

Warning: This doesn't look like the ES-DE scripts folder.

Selected: %1$s

ES-DE may not find the scripts in this location.

Continue anyway?

Warning: This doesn't look like the ES-DE downloaded_media folder.

Selected: %1$s

Artwork may not display correctly.

Continue anyway?

Warning: Required scripts are missing from this folder.

Without these, the app cannot receive game information from ES-DE.

Continue anyway?
Keep the home tab background clear without blur or dimming effects
Disable blur and dimming effects on specific tabs
Show play/pause button when tapping a video. If disabled, video exits.
How videos fit on the display
How system background images fit on the screen
How game background images fit on the screen
Choose which media type to show when browsing systems
Tap to choose an image or GIF
Choose which media type to show when browsing games
Choose which media type to show for the game logo overlay
Drag the logo to reposition it
Use a random image instead of the first one found
Minimum width for narrow/portrait logo images
Size of game artwork when browsing games
Run the setup wizard to configure paths and scripts
Clear ES-DE display and show your system wallpaper
Quickly switch between ES-DE and your chosen wallpaper target
Choose what to toggle to when pressing the wallpaper shortcut
Image used for quick wallpaper switching
GIF used for quick wallpaper switching
Video used for quick wallpaper switching
Screen off on game launch, on when exiting
Keep wallpaper and logo visible when a game is launched
Custom folder for console/system logos (e.g. snes.svg, n64.png)
Custom folder for console/system background images or videos
Use one image file for all console backgrounds
Use one logo file for all consoles
Use one image file for all game backgrounds
Use one logo file for all games
Custom folder for game media (videos, screenshots, fanart, etc.). Overrides the default ES-DE downloaded_media path for the companion screen.
Mute audio when playing a system background video
Restart the video when it reaches the end
What to display when ES-DE screensaver is active
How many apps to show while screensaver is active (All = no limit)
Select music folder. Name files like system logos (e.g., n64.mp3, snes.mp3) or use systems/ subfolders
Play unique music per system. Off = continuous queue of all music
Loop tracks when playlist ends. Off = play once and stop
What to do with music when video plays
Not supported yet. Please use system slider.
Adjust the transparency of the drawer background
Hide apps, docks, and folders when browsing games. UI reappears when returning to system selection.
Play music regardless of background audio from other apps. Music will not pause or duck when other apps play audio.
Select a folder to scan for empty directories
Removes empty folders from your selected directories. Folders containing only systeminfo.txt files will also be removed.
This will delete all empty folders in your selected directories. Folders containing only systeminfo.txt files will also be removed.\n\nThis action cannot be undone.
Filter games to a specific system
Search by game name, system, genre, developer, or publisher
Display your system wallpaper behind ROM search results. When disabled, an OLED-friendly dark background is used instead.
Use pure black for the search screen background, or follow OLED background color when off.
Display your system wallpaper behind ROM search results.
Exclude games with no artwork of any type.
Exclude games with no artwork, descriptions, or other scraped info.
Animate the game with a spin or flip animation when focused. Only discs will spin and Physical Media type must be selected.
Automatically filter by platform when navigating the platform row with a controller.
Show platform logo images in the platform row instead of text labels.
Enable the @android command and plain-text search to include all installed apps.
Choose which artwork type is shown on game cards.
Open ROM Search directly from settings.

Animate disc art with a spin when focused. Applies to PS1, PS2, PS3, Sega CD, Saturn, Dreamcast, GameCube, Wii, Wii U, Xbox, Xbox 360, 3DO, Jaguar CD, CD32, and CDTV. Only active when Physical Media type is selected.
Link each of your ESDE system folders to an Android app for quick access while browsing. Tap a system card to assign or change its linked app.
Please add a ROMs folder to start linking system folders to Android apps.
This will remove the system entry and its settings.
Show only game/system logos with transparent background system wallpaper shows through
Animate the logo bouncing in and out when it shows or hides
Animate the logo bouncing in when switching between systems or games
Link each of your ESDE system folders to an Android app for quick access while browsing. Tap a system card to assign or change its linked app.
Choose when the Konfetti animation plays
Display the first character of the game title as individual Konfetti
Letter or number that particles form before bursting. Leave empty to use the game\'s first letter.
Which animation plays after the letter forms
Create custom Konfetti animations that play when a game is selected or started
You are %1$d steps away from becoming floaty
You are now floaty! Head to Extras to toggle it on.
Apps float around with physics-based bouncing
Play explosion effect when sections are tapped in Settings screens
Use floating overlay positions on the powered off screen
Use floating app layout in the app drawer
Choose how many apps appear while floaty app drawer effect is enabled
Tap bubbles to pop them with haptics; all bubbles return after clearing
Choose which tabs use floaty mode
Turn off and lock it again until version is tapped 7 times
Floaty mode reset. Tap version 7 times to unlock again.
Build your collection of game Jingles
Play a matching Jingle when browsing games in ES-DE
Build your own collection of game jingles
Build a collection of game Jingles from GitHub repositories
Folder must contain index.json and a jingles sub-folder
Folder must contain index.json and a jingles sub-folder
Try regex patterns before exact name. When off, exact name is matched first.
Automatically boost quiet tracks using Android\'s LoudnessEnhancer

The Cocoon frontend allows Jingles to be created and used to easily share audio for playback when browsing games. Those assets can also be used in Jarngreipr! Repositories are hosted by third parties and may contain copyrighted material. Neither Cocoon nor Jarngreipr hosts, verifies, or controls repository content. You are responsible for ensuring you have the rights to download or use repository assets.
1. Create a folder anywhere on your device.
2. Add an index.json file at the root of the folder.
3. Create a jingles subfolder and place your audio files inside.
4. Tap Pick Folder to select your folder.
Fork or use the template below as a starting point, then add it as username/repo-name in the Jingle Repo field.
If Jarngreipr fails to download all tracks, visit the repo directly on GitHub and download the index.json and jingles folder. You can then add this as a Local Folder for playback.
Check your connection and try again
Select the folder where your new jingle pack will be created as a subfolder. You\'ll name the pack on the next screen.
Used as the JSON key and subfolder name
Extension is preserved from the picked file
Play all audio files in a folder in order, looping
Loop a single audio file continuously
Tap + to add your first feed
Why doesn\'t the Now Playing bubble appear?

The Now Playing bubble reads media metadata from other apps via Android's notification listener. It requires Notification Access to be granted once.

Go to Settings → Apps → Special app access → Notification access and enable it for this app.

If you're sideloading and can't reach that screen, you can grant it with a single ADB command — no PC required if you use Termux.
How do I grant Notification Access via ADB (no PC needed)?

You can run ADB commands directly on your Android device using Termux from F-Droid (not the Play Store version).

1. Install Termux from f-droid.org
2. Open Termux and run: pkg update && pkg install android-tools
3. Go to Settings → Developer Options → enable Wireless Debugging
4. Tap "Pair device with pairing code" — note the IP, pairing port, and code shown
5. In Termux: adb pair IP:PAIRPORT then enter the code when prompted
6. Then: adb connect IP:DEBUGPORT (the debug port shown on the main Wireless Debugging screen)
7. Finally run the command below

Notification access persists across reinstalls of this app.
